disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Able to bear weight (4 steps) immediately after injury and now — Ottawa weight-bearing criterion met (low fracture risk)
trigger_question_examples: Could you take four steps on it right after it happened, and can you now?
Are you able to put weight on it and walk, even with a limp?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, I walked home on it and I can take several steps now, just with a limp.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No bony tenderness over the posterior edge/tip of either malleolus, base of the 5th metatarsal, or navicular (Ottawa bony criteria negative)
trigger_question_examples: When you press on the bony point at the back of the ankle knobs, the little-toe base, or the top-inner midfoot bone, is it tender?
Is the pain over the bone or over the soft ligament in front of the ankle knob?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Pressing those bony points isn't tender — the sore spot is the soft ligament just in front of the outer ankle bone.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No distal numbness, no cold/pale foot, no calf tenderness — neurovascular intact
trigger_question_examples: Any numbness in the foot, or is it cold/pale?
Can you feel and wiggle your toes normally?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No numbness, the foot's warm with a normal colour, and I can feel and move my toes fine.
